name: Salary & Income Tax official sources
notes: Salary & Income Tax carries the site's highest-stakes legal figures: PAYE/income-tax bands, personal reliefs, tax-free thresholds and statutory deduction rates (NSSF, SHIF/NHIF, pension, social security). These change with each country's finance act or amendment act, not on a fixed calendar.

Never update a band, relief, threshold or statutory rate because a URL's content hash changed. Open the page and read the gazetted Act or the revenue authority's published notice, exactly as .claude/rules/government.md and .claude/rules/energy.md require. A changed page is not a rate change.
A revenue authority (KRA, FIRS, SARS, GRA, URA, TRA, ...) always outranks a payroll vendor, aggregator or 'net pay calculator' commercial product for the same claim.

The Kenya AHL episode (July 2026) is the cautionary tale for this hub. The 15% affordable-housing RELIEF was repealed by the Tax Laws (Amendment) Act 2024, but the same Act made the Affordable Housing Levy an ALLOWABLE DEDUCTION that reduces taxable income. The flagship ke-paye page had confidently coded (and advertised) the opposite, overstating PAYE for every Kenyan. Read the Act, not the headline.

Only some of the 54 markets have a bound revenue-authority URL here today. The rest are recorded in gaps.revenueAuthoritiesWithoutUrl as known liabilities, not to-dos. Do not delete one without either finding the official URL or showing the figure as planning-grade.

The bug class to watch, per .claude/rules/telecom.md and .claude/rules/energy.md: a per-country band/rate lookup that resolves to `|| 0` turns a missing tax table into 'no tax', which then looks like a great salary. The PAYE engines audited in July 2026 guard this (ke-paye validates the salary; francophone-paye-engine returns {error:'Country not configured'} for an unknown country; dropdowns are populated from the same config object). Any new multi-country tool must keep that pattern — filter the dropdown or force user input, never default a missing table to zero.

sources.title: KRA — Kenya PAYE, tax bands and reliefs
sources.notes: Authoritative for Kenya PAYE bands, personal relief and the treatment of NSSF, SHIF and AHL. Read the current Income Tax Act (Cap 470) and the operative Finance/Tax Laws (Amendment) Act notice before changing a figure. AHL and SHIF are allowable deductions that reduce taxable income (Tax Laws (Amendment) Act 2024, effective 27 Dec 2024).

sources.title: NSSF Kenya — contribution tiers and limits
sources.notes: Authoritative for the NSSF Act 2013 phased contribution schedule (LEL/UEL and the 6% Tier I+II rate). Year 4 rates effective Feb 2026: UEL KES 108,000, max employee/employer contribution KES 6,480/month. Read the gazetted schedule before changing the ceiling.

sources.title: FIRS — Nigeria income tax
sources.notes: Nigeria PAYE is administered by the State Internal Revenue Services under the Personal Income Tax Act (PITA); FIRS is the federal authority and the reference for CIT and the finance-act amendments that flow into PAYE reliefs (CRA, pension, NHF, NHIS). Verify the operative Finance Act before changing bands or the Consolidated Relief Allowance.

sources.title: SARS — rates of tax for individuals
sources.notes: Authoritative for South Africa PAYE brackets, primary/secondary/tertiary rebates, tax thresholds and UIF. Tables are set annually in the Budget; read the current year's rates page before changing a bracket.

sources.title: GRA — PAYE
sources.notes: Authoritative for Ghana PAYE bands and their interaction with SSNIT (Tier 1/2/3) contributions. Bands are amended by the annual Income Tax (Amendment) Act; read it before changing a figure.

sources.title: URA — Pay As You Earn
sources.notes: Authoritative for Uganda PAYE bands (including the additional 10% above UGX 10m/month) and NSSF. Read the Income Tax Act and the operative amendment before changing a band.

sources.title: TRA — PAYE
sources.notes: Authoritative for Tanzania mainland PAYE bands (Zanzibar differs), NSSF/PSSSF and SDL. Read the current Finance Act before changing a band.

sources.title: RRA — Pay As You Earn
sources.notes: Authoritative for Rwanda PAYE bands and RSSB (pension/maternity/medical). The 2023 law phased in new bands; read the operative law before changing a figure.

sources.title: ZRA — Pay As You Earn
sources.notes: Authoritative for Zambia PAYE bands, NAPSA and NHIMA. Bands are set in the annual Budget; read the current year before changing a figure.

sources.title: ZIMRA — PAYE tax tables
sources.notes: Authoritative for Zimbabwe PAYE. Zimbabwe publishes separate ZWL and USD tax tables that change frequently with the currency situation; treat any stored band as high-risk and read the current ZIMRA table before use.

sources.title: MRA — Pay As You Earn
sources.notes: Authoritative for Malawi PAYE bands. Read the current Taxation (Amendment) Act before changing a band.

sources.title: RSL — Pay As You Earn
sources.notes: Authoritative for Lesotho PAYE (two-rate structure plus the annual personal tax credit). Read the current RSL notice before changing a figure.

sources.title: BURS — income tax
sources.notes: Authoritative for Botswana PAYE bands. BURS pages are known to go stale between amendments (see the blog-recovery note); verify against the operative Income Tax Act, not a cached page.

sources.title: NamRA — individual income tax
sources.notes: Authoritative for Namibia PAYE bands and Social Security Commission contributions. Read the current Income Tax Amendment Act before changing a band.

sources.title: ERS — Pay As You Earn
sources.notes: Authoritative for Eswatini PAYE bands and the annual rebate. Read the current ERS notice before changing a figure.

sources.title: MRA Mauritius — PAYE
sources.notes: Authoritative for Mauritius PAYE, the Solidarity Levy and the Contribution Sociale Généralisée (CSG). Mauritius restructured to progressive bands in the 2023/24 Budget; read the current Finance Act before changing a figure.

sources.title: SRC — income tax
sources.notes: Authoritative for Seychelles progressive income tax bands. Read the current SRC notice before changing a figure.

sources.title: ETA — personal income tax
sources.notes: Authoritative for Egypt personal income tax brackets and the personal exemption. Egypt has revised brackets and the exemption several times recently; read the operative law before changing a figure.

revenueAuthoritiesWithoutUrl.note: Ethiopia employment income tax schedule is a bundled planning default with no bound authority URL. Bind mor.gov.et or show the figure as planning-grade.
Sudan PAYE is a bundled planning default with no bound authority URL and the currency situation makes it high-risk. Bind the Taxation Chamber notice or show as planning-grade.
Libya income tax (plus jihad tax) is a bundled planning default with no bound authority URL. Bind the official notice or show as planning-grade.
Angola IRT is a bundled planning default with no bound authority URL. Bind agt.minfin.gov.ao or show as planning-grade.
Mozambique IRPS is a bundled planning default with no bound authority URL. Bind the AT notice or show as planning-grade.
Madagascar IRSA is in engines/francophone-paye-engine.js with the authority named but no bound URL. Bind the DGI notice or show as planning-grade.

revenueAuthoritiesWithoutUrl.authority: DGID (Direction Générale des Impôts et des Domaines)
revenueAuthoritiesWithoutUrl.note: Senegal bands live in the francophone engine; the IPRES/CSS treatment was flagged as a data defect in the Bing SEO push. Bind the DGID source and re-verify before changing.
Côte d'Ivoire ITS/CN bands are in the francophone engine with the authority named but no bound URL. Bind dgi.gouv.ci or show as planning-grade.
Cameroon IRPP bands are in the francophone engine with the authority named but no bound URL. Bind impots.cm or show as planning-grade.
Morocco IR bands are in the francophone engine with the authority named but no bound URL. The 2025 Finance Law revised the brackets; bind tax.gov.ma and re-verify.
Algeria IRG bands are in the francophone engine with the authority named but no bound URL. Bind mfdgi.gov.dz or show as planning-grade.
Tunisia IRPP bands are in the francophone engine; the CNSS treatment was flagged as a data defect in the Bing SEO push. Bind the DGI source and re-verify before changing.
DR Congo IPR bands are in the francophone engine with the authority named but no bound URL. Bind the DGI notice or show as planning-grade.

Equatorial Guinea income tax is a bundled planning default with no bound authority URL. Bind the official notice or show as planning-grade.
Central African Republic IRPP bands are in the francophone engine with the authority named but no bound URL. Bind the DGI notice or show as planning-grade.
Chad IRPP bands are in the francophone engine with the authority named but no bound URL. Bind the DGI notice or show as planning-grade.
Niger ITS bands are in the francophone engine with the authority named but no bound URL. Bind the DGI notice or show as planning-grade.
Mali ITS bands are in the francophone engine with the authority named but no bound URL. Bind the DGI notice or show as planning-grade.
Burkina Faso IUTS bands are in the francophone engine with the authority named but no bound URL. Bind the DGI notice or show as planning-grade.
Guinea RTS bands are in the francophone engine with the authority named but no bound URL. Bind the DNI notice or show as planning-grade.
Guinea-Bissau income tax is a bundled planning default with no bound authority URL. Bind the official notice or show as planning-grade.
Sierra Leone PAYE is a bundled planning default with no bound authority URL. Bind nra.gov.sl or show as planning-grade.
Liberia PAYE is a bundled planning default with no bound authority URL. Bind lra.gov.lr or show as planning-grade.
Mauritania ITS bands are in the francophone engine with the authority named but no bound URL. Bind the DGI notice or show as planning-grade.
Gambia PAYE is a bundled planning default with no bound authority URL. Bind gra.gm or show as planning-grade.

revenueAuthoritiesWithoutUrl.authority: DNRE (Direção Nacional de Receitas do Estado, Cabo Verde)
revenueAuthoritiesWithoutUrl.note: Cabo Verde IUR is a bundled planning default with no bound authority URL. Bind the DNRE notice or show as planning-grade.

revenueAuthoritiesWithoutUrl.authority: Direção dos Impostos (São Tomé e Príncipe)
revenueAuthoritiesWithoutUrl.note: São Tomé income tax is a bundled planning default with no bound authority URL. Bind the official notice or show as planning-grade.
Togo IRPP bands are in the francophone engine with the authority named but no bound URL. Bind otr.tg or show as planning-grade.
Benin IPTS bands are in the francophone engine with the authority named but no bound URL. Bind impots.bj or show as planning-grade.
Burundi IPR bands are in the francophone engine with the authority named but no bound URL. Bind obr.bi or show as planning-grade.
Somalia has a limited PAYE framework and no bound authority URL. Show as planning-grade; do not present a band as a settled national rate.
Djibouti ITS bands are in the francophone engine with the authority named but no bound URL. Bind the DGI notice or show as planning-grade.
Eritrea income tax is a bundled planning default with no bound authority URL. Show as planning-grade.
South Sudan PAYE is a bundled planning default with no bound authority URL. Bind the NRA notice or show as planning-grade.
Comoros IGR bands are in the francophone engine with the authority named but no bound URL. Bind the DGI notice or show as planning-grade.

unsourcedClaims.why: The band tables, personal reliefs and tax-free thresholds for these markets are bundled planning defaults (many in engines/francophone-paye-engine.js) with the revenue authority named but no bound URL. They are estimates for planning, not filing-grade figures. Treat any figure past highRiskCadenceDays as planning-grade and confirm with the authority.

Statutory deduction ceilings (NSSF, pension, social-security upper/lower earnings limits) are the most perishable numbers in this hub — they are re-gazetted on their own schedule, separate from the tax bands. Kenya's NSSF UEL alone moved 18,000 -> 72,000 -> 108,000 across three years. Never carry a ceiling forward without reading the operative schedule.

The Currency & FX tools use live FX via /api/forex with /data/forex/latest.json as fallback. Rates are inherently live; a stored rate is a fallback, never a fixed reference. Not bound to a revenue authority.
